behavioral_changes.summary: The sys_run_metrics persistence contract now includes three new per-run fraction columns: fraction_full, fraction_incremental, and fraction_no_refresh, carried through table DDL, the in-memory row DTO, and generated PySpark schema/data.
RunMetricsTableWriter now computes per-run refresh-policy fractions from terminal nodes only (Completed or Failed), recognizing Full/Incremental/NoRefresh policies with case-insensitive matching.

Fraction computation is defensive: null nodeMetrics, unrecognized or blank policies, and runs with zero eligible terminal nodes leave the fraction fields at 0 instead of dividing by zero or diluting the denominator.

Generated PySpark merge code now treats the fraction values as true doubles by declaring DoubleType columns and serializing each numeric literal as an invariant round-trip Python float(...) expression.
The summary SQL projection now adds fractionFullSum, fractionIncrementalSum, and fractionNoRefreshSum for the current date window, using COALESCE so legacy NULL stored values contribute zero.
The /summary response schema now exposes a refreshPolicyDistribution object with full, incremental, and noRefresh fields.
GetSummaryAsync now maps the new SQL aggregates into refreshPolicyDistribution, rounding each value to 1 decimal and defaulting to zeros for DBNull or empty result sets.

grounding_evidence.reason: Table creation DDL adds the three persisted fraction columns.
Eligibility for the new fractions is defined here: only Completed/Failed nodes with recognized refresh policies, matched case-insensitively.
The denominator guard and the three numerator/denominator calculations implement the fraction values and avoid divide-by-zero.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: sb.AppendLine("from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, LongType, IntegerType, DoubleType");
grounding_evidence.reason: PySpark code generation imports DoubleType specifically to support the new fraction columns.
The generated DataFrame schema now exposes the three fraction columns as nullable doubles.
The three computed fraction values are emitted into the generated PySpark data tuple for MERGE.
Double literals are now wrapped as invariant, round-trip Python float(...) expressions so even 0/1-like doubles remain Python floats.
RunMetricsRow now carries the three new fraction values between collection and PySpark generation.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
SUM(CASE WHEN run_date >= @startDate THEN COALESCE(fraction_full, 0) ELSE 0 END) AS fractionFullSum,
SUM(CASE WHEN run_date >= @startDate THEN COALESCE(fraction_incremental, 0) ELSE 0 END) AS fractionIncrementalSum,
SUM(CASE WHEN run_date >= @startDate THEN COALESCE(fraction_no_refresh, 0) ELSE 0 END) AS fractionNoRefreshSum,
grounding_evidence.reason: The summary query projects the new refresh-policy distribution aggregates and null-proofs stored values with COALESCE.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
[JsonProperty("refreshPolicyDistribution")]
public RefreshPolicyDistribution RefreshPolicyDistribution { get; set; } = new RefreshPolicyDistribution();
grounding_evidence.reason: The summary API contract now includes a non-null refreshPolicyDistribution object.
Defines the three JSON fields exposed under refreshPolicyDistribution.
DB mapping populates refreshPolicyDistribution from the new SQL aliases, rounds each value to 1 decimal, and defaults DBNull to zero.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
var response = new InsightsSummaryResponse();

if (await reader.ReadAsync(cancellationToken))
grounding_evidence.reason: Empty result sets now return a default InsightsSummaryResponse instead of requiring a row to exist.
GetSummaryAsync now uses the new mapping path, making the refreshPolicyDistribution fields part of the live /summary response.

scenario_sketches.title: Compute mixed per-run refresh fractions from eligible terminal nodes
scenario_sketches.rationale: Build a DAG execution with Completed/Failed nodes spanning multiple recognized refresh policies and assert FractionFull/FractionIncremental/FractionNoRefresh are computed as the correct proportions and sum to 1.0 for the eligible run.

scenario_sketches.title: Exclude skipped, blank, unknown, and absent node policies from the denominator
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover three boundaries: eligible nodes mixed with excluded nodes, zero eligible nodes, and nodeMetrics == null. Assert fractions remain zero when nothing is eligible and no divide-by-zero occurs.

scenario_sketches.title: Persist the three fraction columns end-to-end in the run-metrics contract
scenario_sketches.rationale: Assert the table DDL, RunMetricsRow DTO, generated StructType schema, and emitted data tuple all contain fraction_full/fraction_incremental/fraction_no_refresh in the expected contract shape.

scenario_sketches.title: Emit PySpark-safe double literals for zero, integer-valued, and fractional distributions
scenario_sketches.rationale: Verify FormatDouble and BuildPySparkMergeCode serialize 0, 1, and non-integer values as float(...) with invariant '.' formatting and round-trip-safe precision so DoubleType rows are not silently rejected.

scenario_sketches.title: Project refresh-policy distribution sums in the summary SQL
scenario_sketches.rationale: Validate BuildSummaryQuery contains the three fraction sum aliases for the current-period CASE expression so /summary can retrieve the distribution totals from SQL.

scenario_sketches.title: Treat legacy NULL fraction columns as zero in summary aggregation/mapping
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use a reader or integration fixture with null fraction aggregates to confirm the final response reports 0/0/0 rather than nulls or exceptions, matching the COALESCE + IsDBNull behavior.

scenario_sketches.title: Expose refreshPolicyDistribution with full/incremental/noRefresh JSON fields
scenario_sketches.rationale: Serialize or deserialize an InsightsSummaryResponse and assert the payload shape contains refreshPolicyDistribution.full, .incremental, and .noRefresh, with the object present by default.

scenario_sketches.title: Map summary fraction sums with 1-decimal rounding and default object on empty results
scenario_sketches.rationale: Mock DbDataReader for populated values, DBNull values, and no rows; assert MapSummaryResponseAsync rounds to one decimal, returns zeros for DBNull, and still returns a non-null refreshPolicyDistribution when nothing is read.
